name: HiveMQ
what_it_does: Enterprise MQTT platform with a REST API for cloud-managed IoT messaging at scale, with strong MQTT v5 support and persistent sessions.
use_cases: Managing enterprise IoT device fleets requiring guaranteed message delivery and persistent sessions
Configuring and monitoring a fully managed MQTT broker via REST API without infrastructure ops
Routing IoT device telemetry to downstream systems using HiveMQ's data hub integrations
Building agents that monitor MQTT client connectivity and session state at scale
Integrating MQTT messaging into enterprise systems requiring SOC2 and ISO27001 compliance
not_for: Small-scale hobby IoT projects where EMQX open-source is sufficient and free
General-purpose HTTP API messaging without IoT or MQTT requirements
best_when: Best when you need enterprise-grade, fully managed MQTT infrastructure with SLA guarantees, compliance certifications, and a REST management API.
avoid_when: Avoid when budget is constrained — enterprise pricing is significant — or when MQTT is not your required protocol.

auth.notes: HiveMQ Cloud REST API uses API tokens. MQTT connections use credential-based auth or TLS client certificates. Enterprise supports LDAP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition is free self-hosted; HiveMQ Cloud free tier: 100 connections, 10GB/month
pricing.paid_tiers: HiveMQ Cloud from $500/month; Enterprise on-premise from custom pricing
pricing.notes: Community edition is open-source (Apache 2.0). Significant price jump to enterprise/cloud tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Cloud free tier: 100 concurrent connections. Enterprise: contract-defined

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns clear JSON errors with descriptive messages. MQTT protocol error codes follow spec.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: MQTT v5 supports exactly-once delivery (QoS 2). REST management calls vary by endpoint.
known_agent_gotchas: Free cloud tier is limited to 100 connections — easy to hit in testing with multiple agent sessions
Enterprise and Cloud APIs differ; ensure you're referencing the right documentation set
MQTT v5 features like shared subscriptions require explicit broker configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise-grade security with mutual TLS, granular ACL policies, and compliance certifications. Regular third-party security audits.
